key_departures.impact: Led the Cowboys in sacks (8.5) in 2025 despite signing mid-September. Generated 40 pressures and 12 TFLs. New DC Christian Parker's scheme fit concerns drove the decision not to pursue a re-signing. Significant loss off the edge.

Traded for a 2026 third-round pick (pick 92). Had signed a 4yr/$80M deal in 2025. One of the most disruptive interior pass rushers in the NFL. Departure significantly thins IDL depth behind Quinnen Williams and Kenny Clark.
Traded to Titans in a 7th-round pick swap (Cowboys receive pick 218, send pick 225). Depth IDL piece. Combined with Odighizuwa trade, IDL depth is now extremely thin.
Led the Cowboys in tackles but performed poorly as a middle linebacker. No interest from the team in re-signing. Cowboys plan full LB room overhaul. Hole at off-ball LB remains.
7-year Cowboys veteran and 75-game starter. New defensive staff did not support retaining him. Departure helped drive Jalen Thompson signing. Wilson's exit is fully offset by Thompson addition.
Posted only 3.0 sacks and 30 pressures in 2025 after a 10.5-sack season in Washington. Declining production and approaching age 32 made re-signing unappealing. Loss of rotational EDGE depth.
Signed 1-year deal with Dolphins. Was WR4/5 depth behind CeeDee Lamb, George Pickens, and Ryan Flournoy. Minimal impact on need picture.
Not tendered as an RFA, expected to sign with Pittsburgh. Was a valuable reserve C/G. Partially replaced by Matt Hennessy signing.
Not tendered as an RFA. Special teams/depth S. Minimal impact.

contract.notes: Renegotiated from existing GB contract. $13.2M signing bonus. 2026 cap hit restructured down to $5.44M via 4 void years. Per OTC/ESPN Todd Archer.

signings.analysis: Gary was acquired via trade for a 2027 4th-round pick and then had his contract renegotiated from $38M/2yrs unguaranteed down to 2yr/$32M with $16M guaranteed — a meaningful pay cut in exchange for security. He has 46.5 career sacks and produced 7.5 sacks, 20 QB hits and a forced fumble in 2025, starting 15 games. The $16M AAV is below the starter-tier top range but this is a clear multi-year starter investment with real guarantees. Gary has ties to new DC Christian Parker from their time together in Green Bay. However, with Clowney and Fowler departed, the overall EDGE room is thin: Gary + Sam Williams (1yr) + Donovan Ezeiruaku (injured, missing OTAs/minicamp) + James Houston + Tyrus Wheat (camp/ST). Does not fully address EDGE — the team still needs elite pass rush help at both 1st-round picks. EDGE drops from critical tier 1 to a meaningful but still urgent tier 2.

contract.notes: Base deal $33M with incentives up to $36M. $9.7M signing bonus, $22M guaranteed per Spotrac. Per ESPN/Schefter/Archer.

signings.analysis: 3-year, $33M deal with $22M guaranteed is a real investment — this is not a bridge. Thompson started 87 of 99 games across five seasons with Arizona, recorded 95 tackles, 6 PDs, and a forced fumble in 2025. The Cowboys see him as a versatile safety/nickel-rover hybrid. He reunites with new Cowboys secondary coach Ryan Smith from Arizona. With Donovan Wilson departed, Thompson steps directly into a starting role alongside a restructured Malik Hooker. At $11M AAV and $22M guaranteed, the S need is fully addressed for the foreseeable future. PFF ranked Thompson 12th among all safeties in 2025 (83.7 grade) while no Cowboys safety cracked the top 62. Safety drops from tier 1 critical to tier 4.

contract.notes: $6M signing bonus. 2026 and 2027 base salaries both guaranteed. Easy out in 2028. Per OTC/Spotrac/ESPN Archer.

signings.analysis: Williams had a career year in 2025 — 1,201 rushing yards (9th in NFL), 11 TDs, 4.8 YPC, plus elite pass-blocking grades (PFF 97.9% efficiency). Re-signing him before free agency opened proved brilliant as comparable RBs signed for $13-14M/yr. At $8M AAV with two fully-guaranteed seasons, this is superb value. RB was not a pre-FA need; re-signing Williams ensures it won't become one.

contract.notes: Non-exclusive franchise tag. $27.298M fully guaranteed for 2026. Long-term extension negotiations ongoing.

signings.analysis: Franchise tag keeps Pickens for 2026 but does not represent a long-term solution. The Cowboys and Pickens have not reached a multi-year extension, leaving WR1 security only for one season. At $27.3M guaranteed, Pickens will be under contract in 2026, but the franchise tag is a one-year bridge. WR need is low given CeeDee Lamb (elite WR1) and Pickens tagged, but a long-term extension is needed. WR partially addressed short-term only.

contract.notes: 1-year deal reportedly worth $5M. Exact guarantees not publicly reported.

signings.analysis: One-year deal adds secondary safety depth. Locke played under new Cowboys DC Christian Parker for three seasons in Denver and offers positional flexibility — played 681 snaps as free safety and 326 in the box and 123 as nickel over two seasons per PFF. He's primarily a depth/special teams piece, having started 15 games in 2024. His connection to Parker and the scheme is the real value here. Does not change the S need tier (Thompson's signing already fully addresses it).

contract.notes: 1-year deal worth a maximum of $3M. Per ESPN Todd Archer.

signings.analysis: Former 2nd-round pick returning on a 1-year, low-cost deal after recovering from a torn ACL. Had 1 sack, 25 pressures, and 6 TFLs in 2025 while starting 5 games. He had 8.5 sacks in his first two NFL seasons before the injury. This is a prove-it deal under a new scheme with DC Parker — not a need-solver. With Clowney and Fowler gone, Williams is depth/rotational, not a starter-level fix. EDGE remains a pressing need.

contract.notes: 1-year deal, maximum value $3M per ESPN and CBS Sports reports.

signings.analysis: A 1-year, depth signing meant to provide some bodies behind Quinnen Williams and Kenny Clark after the Odighizuwa and Thomas trades. Does not address the IDL depth void created by trading two players. With Ogbonnia, Jay Toia (2025 draft pick), and Clark/Q. Williams, IDL has a starter tier but thin depth. The need for an IDL contributor in the draft remains.

contract.notes: 1-year deal worth up to $5.5M. Per CBS Sports tracker.

signings.analysis: Durant started 39 of 61 games in four seasons with the Rams, recording 7 INTs including 2 pick-sixes. In 2025 he had 3 INTs and 7 pass breakups in the postseason alone (most INTs in the 2025 playoffs). Can play outside CB and nickel. The 1-year structure is a prove-it deal, not a long-term investment. The Cowboys CB room is still injury-prone: DaRon Bland (foot surgery), Shavon Revel Jr. (ACL recovery), and Caelen Carson all have health questions. Durant adds meaningful competition and starter ability but the $5.5M max contract signals depth/bridge tier. CB need partially addressed, still a priority in the draft.

contract.notes: 1-year deal. Financial terms not publicly reported. Depth-level deal.

signings.analysis: Hennessy replaces the depth hole left by Brock Hoffman (not tendered, headed to Pittsburgh). Can play center and guard, with starting experience in Atlanta. This is a depth signing — not a starter solution. T.J. Bass (RFA tendered) and Tyler Smith remain the interior starter core. The signing minimally addresses IOL depth but doesn't move the needle on IOL as a draft need.

contract.notes: 1-year deal. Terms not disclosed. Likely veteran minimum or near it.

signings.analysis: QB was not a need — Dak Prescott, Joe Milton III, and Will Grier are all under contract. Howell (5-13 career record, 22 TDs/23 INTs) is a 4th-arm for the offseason program, not a serious depth concern. This signing does not address any meaningful need.

Undrafted UDFA returner who primarily plays special teams. Logged only 66 defensive snaps for Detroit in 2025 (215 ST snaps). Had 1.5 sacks and a forced fumble on limited work. At $1.755M this is essentially a minimum deal for ST depth and camp competition. Does not address the EDGE starter need at all.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Rashan Gary trade (2yr/$32M, $16M gtd) adds a legitimate multi-year starter with 46.5 career sacks and 7.5 in 2025. However, Clowney (8.5 sacks, team-best) and Fowler (3.0 sacks) both departed. Donovan Ezeiruaku will miss OTAs and minicamp after hip surgery. The EDGE room is Gary + Sam Williams (1yr prove-it) + Ezeiruaku (injured) + James Houston + Tyrus Wheat (ST). The Cowboys still hold picks 12 and 20 and EDGE remains a strong two-pick target. Need drops from critical Tier 1 to urgent Tier 2 — still a premium draft priority.

Jalen Thompson signing (3yr/$33M, $22M gtd) is a real multi-year investment at starter-tier AAV ($11M/yr is well within S starter range). Thompson is a 5-year starter (87 starts in 99 games), versatile, and in his prime at 27. Paired with a restructured Malik Hooker and depth from P.J. Locke (1yr/$5M), the safety position is solidly addressed. No longer a draft need; drop to tier 4.

Kenneth Murray Jr. (starter, team-leading tackler) departed with no interest from the team. The Cowboys struck out on both Quay Walker and Nakobe Dean (both went to Raiders). DeMarvion Overshown is the only returning starting LB; Shemar James (rookie) and Marist Liufau (converting to OLB/EDGE under Parker) are the only other bodies. Zero free agent LB signings. LB is now the single biggest unaddressed hole on the roster — escalated to Tier 1 critical.

Trading Odighizuwa (one of the most disruptive interior pass rushers in the NFL) and Solomon Thomas stripped the depth behind Quinnen Williams and Kenny Clark. Ogbonnia (1yr/$3M) is a depth-only addition. Jay Toia (2025 rookie) is the only other interior piece. IDL depth is now critically thin and the Cowboys need to address interior DL in the draft — particularly a rotational pass rusher to play next to Williams and Clark in Parker's new 3-4 base. Escalated from tier 3 to tier 2.

Cobie Durant (1yr/$5.5M) adds a starter-capable CB to an injury-plagued room but on a prove-it contract with no long-term commitment. DaRon Bland is rehabbing foot surgery, Shavon Revel is one year removed from ACL, and Caelen Carson was injured in 2025. Durant's addition slightly reduces urgency but the health uncertainty keeps CB at Tier 2. Cowboys should still pursue CB in the first two rounds given the injury risk concentration.

Brock Hoffman's departure (not tendered) is partially replaced by Matt Hennessy (1yr depth). T.J. Bass (RFA tendered 2nd round) and Tyler Smith remain solid starters. Hennessy adds experience but is a depth piece. IOL is a modest need for depth/developmental — tier 3 unchanged.
Javonte Williams re-signed on a 3yr/$24M deal with $16M guaranteed. Coming off 1,201-yard, 11-TD season. RB is fully locked up — not a draft consideration at any meaningful round.

George Pickens franchise-tagged ($27.3M, fully guaranteed for 2026) alongside CeeDee Lamb (elite WR1 under contract). WR depth is thin after Tolbert's departure to Miami but the starter pairing is intact for 2026. Long-term WR extension needed for Pickens or this reasserts as a need in 2027. For the 2026 draft, WR is not a pressing need — drop to tier 4.

key_additions.headline: Pro Bowl edge rusher (46.5 career sacks, 7.5 in 2025) acquired for a 2027 4th-round pick to anchor pass rush rebuild under DC Christian Parker
Five-year starter (87 starts) fills Cowboys' biggest secondary void on a real multi-year investment, reuniting with secondary coach Ryan Smith
Career-best 1,201-yard, 11-TD season locked up long-term on below-market deal before RB prices soared in free agency
Breakout WR retained on franchise tag while long-term extension talks continue, keeping elite Lamb-Pickens receiver duo intact for 2026
NFL's postseason interceptions leader (3 in 2025 playoffs) bolsters injury-depleted CB room on a one-year deal
Versatile safety/nickel hybrid reunites with DC Christian Parker from Denver, adding depth and special teams value
Former 2nd-round pick re-signed on prove-it deal after returning from ACL; upside candidate under new pass-rush-focused scheme
Interior depth signing provides bodies behind Quinnen Williams and Kenny Clark after dual IDL trade departures
Versatile C/G depth replaces Brock Hoffman's interior line reserve role after Hoffman was not tendered
Undrafted UDFA returner adds special teams value and camp competition at EDGE on a near-minimum deal
4th QB arm added for offseason program; no threat to Dak Prescott's depth chart standing

key_losses.headline: Cowboys' 2025 sack leader (8.5) let walk as scheme-fit concerns under new DC Parker trumped production; EDGE room remains a glaring need
One of the NFL's most disruptive interior pass rushers traded away despite $80M deal signed in 2025, creating immediate IDL depth crisis
Interior depth piece traded to Tennessee, compounding IDL thinning after the Odighizuwa deal
Starting linebacker and team-leading tackler not re-signed, leaving LB as the single biggest unaddressed hole entering the draft
7-year Cowboys veteran and 75-game starter let go as new defensive staff sought a younger, more athletic safety — fully replaced by Thompson
Disappointing 3.0-sack season (after 10.5 in 2024) cost him a return; his departure deepens an already-thin EDGE room

key_losses.contract_summary: Not tendered (RFA) — headed to Pittsburgh Steelers
key_losses.headline: Reliable reserve C/G not tendered, leaving a depth hole partially filled by Matt Hennessy signing
WR depth departure after losing starting opportunities to Ryan Flournoy; minimal impact given Lamb and Pickens on roster

notes: Contract figures sourced from OverTheCap, Spotrac, and ESPN's Todd Archer reports. Jalen Thompson's base deal confirmed at $33M/$22M guaranteed (Spotrac/Bleacher Report/ESPN), with incentives to $36M (NFL Network/Yahoo). Rashan Gary's renegotiated deal confirmed at 2yr/$32M/$16M gtd (OTC/Spotrac/Heavy). Javonte Williams' deal confirmed at 3yr/$24M/$16M gtd (OTC/Spotrac/NFL.com). Matt Hennessy and Sam Howell financial terms not publicly disclosed — minimum/depth tier assumed. Tyrus Wheat contract value sourced at $1.755M from El-Balad/CBS reports. Clowney, Dante Fowler, Kenneth Murray, and Donovan Wilson status listed as unsigned UFA as of March 16, 2026. Cowboys hold 2026 draft picks 12 and 20 (both first-rounders via Micah Parsons trade) plus 2026 pick 92 (from Odighizuwa/49ers trade).
